page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r7.2xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 8 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8369B processor running at 2.7 GHz. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps.

Benchmark results place this instance consistently in the average tier for most operations. The Intel Xeon processor achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 1476.0 and a multi-core score of 5558.0. Memory bandwidth is measured at 420.64 GB/sec for cached small-block reads, dropping to 56.43 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression is a relative bottleneck, scoring in the bottom 25% at 35.52 GB/sec. For lightweight AI workloads, the instance handles prompt processing for a small 135M LLM at 711.18 tokens/sec, but performance drops significantly to a poor rating of 14.73 tokens/sec when processing a medium 7B model.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.r7.2xlarge offers a balanced resource density for memory-bound workloads without the overhead of local NVMe storage or GPU accelerators. It is designed for memory-intensive applications that do not require high-performance local disk I/O. Appropriate workloads include Redis caching layers, where it achieves 7.77 million SET operations per second, relational database hosting, static web serving, and moderate-scale software compilation.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r7.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon 8369B vCPUs running at 2.7 GHz and 64.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, this x86_64 server provides 8.0 GB of memory per core but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks place the instance in the average tier for most operations, including a Geekbench multi-core score of 5558.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 11208.37. Memory read bandwidth reaches 420.64 GB/sec for cached small blocks. While it exhibits weaker multi-threaded decompression and poor medium-model LLM inference speeds, it maintains steady throughput for memory-bound tasks. This profile makes the instance qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring high memory capacity relative to compute power, such as Redis caching, database operations, and static web serving.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r7.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and 5 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering average benchmark performance for databases and caching.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r7.2xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 64 GB RAM, and KVM virtualization for database workloads.
tagline: An x86_64 memory-optimized instance featuring eight dedicated Intel Xeon cores and sixty-four gigabytes of RAM for memory-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated Intel Xeon 8369B vCPUs running at 2.7 GHz
64.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
Baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps with no local storage
Average performance in database operations and Redis SET transactions
Best suited for memory-intensive caching, databases, and web serving
